children.text: There is hereby authorized to be appropriated, from time to time, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, such amounts as may be necessary to carry out the provisions and to accomplish the purposes of said conventions and of this subchapter and regulations made pursuant thereto, and the Secretary of the Interior is authorized out of such moneys to employ in the city of Washington and elsewhere such persons and means as he may deem necessary for such purpose and may cooperate with local authorities in the protection of migratory birds and make the necessary investigations connected therewith.

children.tail: ; amended 1939 Reorg. Plan No. II, § 4(f), eff.
, provided in part that this section is effective as of the day aforesaid (
). See Effective Date of 1936 Amendment note set out under

children.text: “That all moneys now or hereafter available for administration and enforcement of said Act approved

children.tail: [this subchapter], shall be equally available for the administration and enforcement of said Act as hereby amended.”
